hyperedges.label: LocalStorage-first Persistence Strategy (streak, history, offline routine, cloud mirror)
Automatic Progression Flow (history → summary → prescription → bias → badge)

nodes.label: Layered Backend Architecture (Routes → Controllers → Services → Repositories)
nodes.rationale: Business logic lives in services and is framework-agnostic; dependency injection happens at composition time in routineRoutes.ts, keeping each layer independently testable.
nodes.norm_label: layered backend architecture (routes → controllers → services → repositories)

nodes.label: Repository Pattern (IExerciseRepository)
nodes.rationale: Exercises are served from static JSON behind an IExerciseRepository interface so swapping in MongoDB (Phase 3) means writing one new repository class; the service layer does not change — a painless DB migration by design.

nodes.label: Zod Input Validation at Every Endpoint
nodes.rationale: Every endpoint validates its payload against a schema (userProfile.schema.ts) before it reaches business logic, replacing manual field checks.
nodes.norm_label: zod input validation at every endpoint

nodes.label: Pure Functions for Testability
nodes.rationale: Core logic (e.g. frontend/src/lib/history.ts) is written as pure functions precisely so it can be tested without mocking React or Express; 34 Vitest tests cover generation, progression and history logic.

nodes.label: Custom Hooks for Separation of Concerns (useWorkout, useStreak, useRestTimer, useHistory)
nodes.rationale: Custom hooks keep App.tsx declarative and make each concern independently testable.
nodes.norm_label: custom hooks for separation of concerns (useworkout, usestreak, useresttimer, usehistory)

nodes.label: Offline Mode via LocalStorage
nodes.rationale: The active routine survives refreshes and connection loss via LocalStorage, with emergency client-side generation if the server is unreachable — the app must work inside a gym with bad connectivity.

nodes.label: Re-roll Mechanic (swap exercise, same muscle group)
nodes.norm_label: re-roll mechanic (swap exercise, same muscle group)

nodes.label: Smart Starting Weights (bodyweight/sex/experience strength ratios, 2.5 kg rounding)
nodes.norm_label: smart starting weights (bodyweight/sex/experience strength ratios, 2.5 kg rounding)

nodes.label: Shared Domain Types (WorkoutRoutine / RoutineSet end-to-end)
nodes.rationale: The WorkoutRoutine / RoutineSet contracts are typed end to end in TypeScript on both sides of the API, preventing frontend/backend contract drift.

nodes.label: BurnOut — Generador Inteligente de Rutinas (Spanish README)
nodes.author: Mario Minuesa (@Mariomin23)
nodes.norm_label: burnout — generador inteligente de rutinas (spanish readme)

nodes.label: Fit-PokéAPI Product Vision (instant gamified gym routine generation)
nodes.rationale: The user arrives at the gym not knowing what to do; the app must generate an instant, complete, optimized and gamified routine (PokéAPI-style) from split + goal + biophysical profile.
nodes.norm_label: fit-pokeapi product vision (instant gamified gym routine generation)

nodes.label: Re-roll: swap exercise within same muscle group without repeating
nodes.norm_label: re-roll: swap exercise within same muscle group without repeating

nodes.label: Exercise Data Contract (id, name, target_muscle, split_category, youtube_video_url, difficulty, equipment)
nodes.norm_label: exercise data contract (id, name, target_muscle, split_category, youtube_video_url, difficulty, equipment)

nodes.label: RoutineSet Data Contract (per-set tracking: reps, weight, RPE)
nodes.norm_label: routineset data contract (per-set tracking: reps, weight, rpe)

nodes.label: Multi-Agent Orchestration (CAFYD fitness, UX/UI, Gamification, SEO sub-agents)
nodes.rationale: Code is structured into clean modules and interfaces so specialized sub-agents can audit and extend without conflicts.
nodes.norm_label: multi-agent orchestration (cafyd fitness, ux/ui, gamification, seo sub-agents)

nodes.label: Ember Ledger Design Philosophy
nodes.rationale: The aesthetics of effort made legible: repetition (tally, tick, incremented row) as the most honest visual language; dark industrial space; embers-on-charcoal palette where color intensity encodes magnitude; stepped (never smooth) progress; one monumental gesture against a field of small perfect marks.

nodes.label: ConfirmModal (replaces window.confirm)
nodes.rationale: window.confirm blocks the UI thread on mobile and does not respect the app theme; a custom glass modal with danger action fixes both.

nodes.label: ExerciseCardSkeleton with Shimmer Animation
nodes.rationale: Mirrors the real ExerciseCard layout (header + 3 set rows) during /generate fetch instead of a pulse spinner, reducing perceived latency.

nodes.label: Difficulty Filtering in Routine Generation
nodes.rationale: beginner → only beginner, intermediate → beginner|intermediate, advanced → all; if the filtered pool has fewer than 3 candidates for a muscle group it widens to the next level — the system never returns an empty routine.

nodes.label: Real Consecutive-Days Streak (fit_poke_streak_v2)
nodes.rationale: Compares date only (no time) to avoid the midnight edge case; uses a new localStorage key (fit_poke_streak_v2) to avoid conflict with the previous raw-number format; streak breaks if a day is skipped.

nodes.label: Hook Extraction from Monolithic App.tsx (useWorkout, useStreak, useRestTimer)
nodes.rationale: App.tsx was a 415-line monolith; extracting three hooks reduces it to ~80-130 lines of view routing and makes state logic independently testable.
nodes.norm_label: hook extraction from monolithic app.tsx (useworkout, usestreak, useresttimer)

nodes.label: UserProfileSchema (Zod) for API Payload Validation
nodes.rationale: Controller validates before calling the service; invalid input returns 400 with structured ZodError details instead of crashing.

nodes.label: crypto.randomUUID() for Routine IDs
nodes.rationale: Replaces Math.random().toString(36) — native in Node 14.17+, zero dependencies, collision-safe.

nodes.label: Fase 2A Design Spec — Automatic Progression + Workout History
nodes.norm_label: fase 2a design spec — automatic progression + workout history

nodes.label: ProgressionService (pure backend prescription engine)
nodes.rationale: Progression is computed in the backend (business logic in services) so that in Fase 3 only the history source changes (Mongo); the class is pure with no I/O, making it directly unit-testable.

nodes.label: Double Progression Auto-regulated by RPE
nodes.rationale: Chosen because it is auto-regulated, standard in hypertrophy training, and exploits the RPE the user already logs. Rules: all sets at range top AND avg RPE ≤ 8 → +weight; any set below range min OR avg RPE ≥ 9.5 → −weight; otherwise keep weight and target best reps + 1. Increment 5 kg if weight_factor ≥ 1.2 else 2.5 kg; 2.5 kg floor; goal change resets reps to new range minimum.

nodes.label: Workout History in localStorage (fit_poke_history_v1, WorkoutLog[], FIFO cap 100)
nodes.rationale: Stored client-side because there is no auth or DB until Fase 3 and Render has an ephemeral filesystem; workouts with 0 completed sets are not saved; exerciseName is kept so logs survive DB changes.
nodes.norm_label: workout history in localstorage (fit_poke_history_v1, workoutlog[], fifo cap 100)

nodes.label: ExerciseHistorySummary Compact Transport (last session per exercise, max 30)
nodes.rationale: A compact summary travels in each /generate and /reroll request: one roundtrip, backend stays stateless, payload is bounded (max 30 entries, 10 sets each).
nodes.norm_label: exercisehistorysummary compact transport (last session per exercise, max 30)

nodes.label: 70/30 Selection Bias Toward Exercises with History
nodes.rationale: Without repeating exercises the progression would never act; 70% bias keeps continuity while 30% randomness preserves novelty. Re-roll stays 100% random because asking for a change expresses wanting variety.
nodes.norm_label: 70/30 selection bias toward exercises with history

nodes.label: Tolerant History Parse (invalid history ignored, never 400)
nodes.rationale: history is validated with a separate safeParse; if invalid the backend logs a warning and falls back — stale data on a user's phone must never break routine generation.
nodes.norm_label: tolerant history parse (invalid history ignored, never 400)

nodes.label: GOAL_REP_RANGE (Perder Peso 12–15, Volumen 8–12, Mantenerse Activo 10–12)
nodes.rationale: suggestedReps moves from a fixed value to a range: start at the minimum and progress toward the top before adding weight.

nodes.label: Progression Direction Badge (↑ up / = keep / ↓ down)
nodes.norm_label: progression direction badge (↑ up / = keep / ↓ down)

nodes.label: Fase 2A Implementation Plan (10 tasks, TDD, completed 2026-07-02)
nodes.norm_label: fase 2a implementation plan (10 tasks, tdd, completed 2026-07-02)

nodes.label: Fase 2B Design Spec — Extended Profile and Progress Charts
nodes.norm_label: fase 2b design spec — extended profile and progress charts

nodes.label: Pure SVG ProgressChart (no chart library)
nodes.rationale: Hand-rolled SVG line chart (~320x150 viewBox, emerald→sky gradient from Ember Ledger) keeps the bundle small (ultra-fast-load principle) and gives total style control.

nodes.label: HistoryView (aggregate stats + per-exercise chart + workout list)
nodes.rationale: 100% frontend: the history already lives in localStorage via useHistory; without auth (Fase 3) history is per-device, so the backend does not participate.
nodes.norm_label: historyview (aggregate stats + per-exercise chart + workout list)

nodes.label: Local-State Navigation (view: home | history, no router)
nodes.rationale: Two views do not justify adding a router dependency; navigation is a local state field in App.
nodes.norm_label: local-state navigation (view: home | history, no router)

nodes.label: Top-Set Chart Metric (max weight per session; max reps if bodyweight)
nodes.rationale: Chart plots the session top set; for bodyweight exercises (all 0 kg) it automatically switches to max reps. Sessions ordered by date act as a proxy for weeks with real date labels.
nodes.norm_label: top-set chart metric (max weight per session; max reps if bodyweight)

nodes.label: Fase 3 Design Spec — MongoDB, JWT Auth and PWA
nodes.norm_label: fase 3 design spec — mongodb, jwt auth and pwa

nodes.label: HybridExerciseRepository (Mongo with static-JSON fallback)
nodes.rationale: Uses Mongo when connected and the collection has data, otherwise falls back to static JSON; the service layer did not change a single line — the swap promised by the repository pattern.

nodes.label: Idempotent Seed — JSON stays the source of truth (syncExercisesToDb)
nodes.rationale: On every startup with a connection, idempotent upserts from exercises.json update the DB: a deploy with new exercises updates the database on its own; manual npm run seed also exists.
nodes.norm_label: idempotent seed — json stays the source of truth (syncexercisestodb)

nodes.label: Stateless JWT Auth (30-day HS256, bcryptjs salt 10)
nodes.rationale: No refresh tokens: for a personal gym app, monthly re-login is acceptable. Login and register return uniform 401/409 without leaking which emails exist.
nodes.norm_label: stateless jwt auth (30-day hs256, bcryptjs salt 10)

nodes.label: Cloud History as Mirror of Local (mergeHistories, local wins)
nodes.rationale: localStorage remains the primary truth so the app works 100% anonymous and offline; on login remote history is merged (dedup by id, local wins, ordered by date, cap 100) and every local change is uploaded whole via PUT to a single per-user document — simple, idempotent, no server-side merge conflicts.
nodes.norm_label: cloud history as mirror of local (mergehistories, local wins)

nodes.label: requireDb Middleware (503 instead of crash without DB)
nodes.rationale: Auth/history endpoints return 503 when the DB is unavailable so the Render deploy keeps working even if MONGO_URI/JWT_SECRET env vars are missing.
nodes.norm_label: requiredb middleware (503 instead of crash without db)

nodes.label: Installable PWA (vite-plugin-pwa, generateSW, autoUpdate)
nodes.rationale: Precaches the full build plus Google Fonts at runtime; the API is deliberately NOT cached because the offline routine mode is already solved by useWorkout's local fallback.

nodes.label: Fase 4 Design Spec — Advanced Gamification and SEO/SEM
nodes.norm_label: fase 4 design spec — advanced gamification and seo/sem

nodes.label: Gamification Engine derived from History (lib/gamification.ts, pure)
nodes.rationale: Everything is derived from the existing workout history so nothing new needs persisting except the best streak, added backward-compatibly to the existing useStreak storage (fit_poke_streak_v2.best).
nodes.norm_label: gamification engine derived from history (lib/gamification.ts, pure)

nodes.label: 21 Medals in 4 Groups (volume, workouts, streaks, mastery)
nodes.norm_label: 21 medals in 4 groups (volume, workouts, streaks, mastery)

nodes.label: XP Levels (XP = workouts×100 + volume/250×25 + mastered×150 + bestStreak×30; 11 titles)
nodes.norm_label: xp levels (xp = workouts×100 + volume/250×25 + mastered×150 + beststreak×30; 11 titles)

nodes.label: Static Exercise SEO Pages (generate-seo-pages.mjs → dist/ejercicios/<slug>/)
nodes.rationale: 150 self-contained static HTML pages generated after vite build avoid migrating the SPA to Next/Astro; Vercel serves them as static files (filesystem wins over the SPA). Slugs are accent-stripped names with id suffix on collision; the backend's exercises.json remains the only source of truth and a missing file warns without breaking the build.

nodes.label: JSON-LD on Exercise Pages (ExercisePlan + BreadcrumbList, canonical, Open Graph)
nodes.norm_label: json-ld on exercise pages (exerciseplan + breadcrumblist, canonical, open graph)

nodes.label: Frontend README (React + TypeScript + Vite template notes)
nodes.norm_label: frontend readme (react + typescript + vite template notes)

nodes.label: SPA HTML Shell (index.html) with SEO metadata and canonical burnout.minuesa.es
nodes.norm_label: spa html shell (index.html) with seo metadata and canonical burnout.minuesa.es

nodes.label: User Profile Inputs (Genero, Peso, Altura, Edad, Experiencia)
nodes.norm_label: user profile inputs (genero, peso, altura, edad, experiencia)

nodes.label: Bloque Anatomico (Split) Selector: Superior / Inferior / Full Body
nodes.norm_label: bloque anatomico (split) selector: superior / inferior / full body

nodes.label: Objetivo Principal Selector: Definir / Hipertrofia / Salud
nodes.norm_label: objetivo principal selector: definir / hipertrofia / salud

nodes.label: Dark Mobile-First UI with Teal/Cyan Gradient Accents
nodes.norm_label: dark mobile-first ui with teal/cyan gradient accents

hyperedges.label: LocalStorage-first Persistence Strategy (streak, history, offline routine, cloud mirror)
Automatic Progression Flow (history → summary → prescription → bias → badge)
